entry.title: Re: How can I connect my SAP BTP Kyma with my On-Premise...
entry.content:
Hi @Aji , Good Day. Resurrecting this after a year as we have stumbled at the same roadblock. This link redirects us to the the connectivity proxy. However, there's no much documentation towards extending JCO to be used in the Kyma runtime. There's no specification on how the SAPJCO calls need to be routed via the connectivity proxy. Nor does SAPJCO seem to natively support proxying.

We have been able to proxy the requests using tools like nginx's tcp routing but are stuck with connectivity-proxy throwing error "com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.server.proxy.InvalidHandshakePropertiesException: Destination host name not set " even though SAP JCO passes the "ashost" parameter. That 's the closest we have gotten. 

We know the code works as it is successful on premise. We have also tried with hardcoding the destination parameters and at the same time configuring the destination as an RFC destination and using the SAP Cloud SDK to fetch destination details.

FYI : We seem to have all the other components setup correctly eg: Cloud Connector with connectivity to on-premise , Connectivity Proxy module in Kyma, RFC destination. 

We are reaching a point where we might need to consider a change in architecture but we would prefer not to if possible.

Something like Kyma - HTTP -> Cloud Connector - HTTP -> Some new custom App in CloudFoundry on the lines on https://github.com/skye0402/jco-rfc-rest-server  - RFC -> Connectivity Service -> Cloud Connector -> On Prem . Since there seems to be a lot more doco for Cloud Foundry to on prem communication via RFC. 

If you or any other Kyma experts on the forum have any guidance towards how to proceed invoking RFC protocol'd function modules using SAPJCO, it would be most appreciated. We suspect that its some configuration that we are missing or its just that SAP JCO is not supported or we are just integrating the wrong components together.

entry.title: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content:
Hi,

I have following code:

 

for( int i = 0; i < 5; i++ )
{
catalog[i].TargetType = SQL_C_WCHAR;
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( bufferSize + 1 );
catalog[i].TargetValuePtr = malloc( sizeof( unsigned char ) * catalog[i].BufferLength );
ret = SQLBindCol( m_hstmt, (SQLUSMALLINT) i + 1, catalog[i].TargetType, catalog[i].TargetValuePtr, catalog[i].BufferLength, &( catalog[i].StrLen_or_Ind ) );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, STMT_ERROR );
result = 1;
break;
}
}

 

This code fails with HY090.

 

However it didn't fail with any other DBMS.

 

Im running SQL Anywhere 17.0 with the driver 17.0.

 

Any idea?

 

Thank you.

Error in entity 'ZTMP_C_GL_HIER2(CDS)': Hierarchy for view ZTMP_C_GL_HIER2 is not supported ZTMP_C_GL_HIER_SB (Service Binding) '


If I replace the 'entity.name' with parentNodeElement:  and nodeElement: option, I am not able the output in a Hierarchal format via preview in Service Binding. 
My codes as follows:
1) For DEFINE HIERARCHY

define hierarchy ztmp_gl_hier_def

as parent child hierarchy (

child to parent association _Parent

start where ParentNode is initial

siblings order by HierarchyNode ascending

)

{

key HierarchyNode,

ParentNode

}

@OData.hierarchy.recursiveHierarchy: [{

define view ztmp_c_gl_hier2 as select from ztmp_gl_hier

association [1..*] to ztmp_c_gl_hier2 as _Parent

@UI:{ selectionField: [{ position: 20 }],

lineItem: [{ position: 20 }] }

key HierarchyNode,

@UI: selectionField: [{ position: 40 }],

lineItem: [{ position: 40 }] }

ParentNode,

_Parent

}

entry.title: Relationship between CBC tenant and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants - Is it 1:1 or 1:N?
entry.content:
**Title:**
Relationship between CBC tenant and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants - Is it 1:1 or 1:N?

**Body:**

Hello SAP Community,

I'm trying to understand the relationship between the CBC (Central Business Configuration) tenant and the various S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants throughout the implementation lifecycle.

**Background:**
From my understanding, the landscape includes the following tenants:

**During Starter Phase:**
- 1 CBC Tenant
- Starter Customizing Tenant (100)
- Starter Development Tenant (080)

**After 3-System Landscape Provisioning:**
- 1 CBC Tenant (same as above?)
- Development Customizing Tenant (100)
- Development Development Tenant (080)
- Test Tenant
- Production Tenant

**My Question:**
Is the CBC tenant relationship to these S/4HANA tenants **1:1** or **1:N**?

Specifically:
1. Does **one CBC tenant** manage configuration for **all five** S/4HANA tenants (Starter Customizing, Starter Development, Dev Customizing, Dev Development, Test, Production)?
2. Or does each S/4HANA tenant have its **own dedicated CBC tenant**?
3. When transitioning from Starter to the 3-system landscape, is it the same CBC tenant or a new one?

**Why this matters:**
I need to understand:
- Whether I create separate CBC projects within one CBC tenant for each target tenant
- Or if there are multiple CBC tenant instances
- How CBC orchestrates configuration across these six different tenants

Any clarification with documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026: What's New in Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
The 1H 2026 release brings 14 features to Employee Central Payroll, covering extensibility improvements, AI-powered pay statement capabilities, Payroll Control Center enhancements, expanded replication support, and legal changes across multiple countries and regions. Here is a detailed breakdown of every feature in this release.

Basic Extensibility for Infotype Actions (0000)

You can now use basic extensibility to replicate custom fields from Employee Central to the Actions infotype (0000) in Employee Central Payroll. Previously, achieving this required implementing the HRSFEC_B_CE_CHANGE_IT0000 BAdI or using advanced extensibility. With this release, you can configure custom field mappings through the standard configuration interface with no custom coding required.

For customers on HCM for SAP S/4HANA, this is particularly relevant because the HRSFEC_B_CE_CHANGE_IT0000 BAdI is listed as unsupported in the simplification list (SI35: HCM_ECP_BAdIs) and business impact note 3394928. Previously, using it required requesting an exception. This standard extensibility option removes that dependency entirely.

Type: New | Enablement: Customer configured

Prerequisite: Install the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Enhancements to Payroll Control Center: Manage Payroll Activities

The Payroll Control Center: Manage Payroll Activities solution receives several enhancements in this release. Note that this solution is only available in the SAP S/4HANA HCM (S4HCM101) system.

Archiving Function: You can now use the archiving object HR: Payroll Control Center Activity List (PCC_ACL) to archive the audit trail of payroll activity list recurrences. This integrates with SAP Information Lifecycle Management. The write program PYC_ARCHIVE_ACL_WRI reads audit trail data that has fulfilled the residency period and writes it to archive files. The read program PYC_ARCHIVE_ACL_READ reads the archived data.

Activity Audit Trail App: A new app lets you check logs for payroll activity list recurrences, activities, steps, and alerts.

Synchronous Update of Employee Selection: Employee data frequently changes during the payroll cycle due to organizational changes, transfers, terminations, and data corrections. These changes can cause stored employee selections in payroll activities to become outdated. The system now continuously monitors for such changes and automatically updates employee selections for ongoing activities. You can configure update frequency and scope, and the system provides notifications when manual review is recommended.

Role-Based Permission for Off-Cycle Payrolls: A new permission, Manage Off-Cycle Payrolls, controls access to the Manage Off-Cycle Payrolls app.

Transport Request for Plan Payroll Activities: A new checkbox in General Settings in the Admin Transaction Report (PYT_ADMIN_TRANSACTION) requires users to specify a transport request when creating activity list recurrences.

Type: Changed | Classification: Major | Enablement: Customer configured

Prerequisite: Install the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Enhancements to AI-Assisted Explain My Pay Statement

Joule-powered pay statement explanations are now available for two additional countries. Employees in Brazil can use Joule in Portuguese, and employees in Canada can use it in English and French. Employees can ask Joule to explain terms or amounts on their most recent pay statement or compare it with the previous statement. Explanations include wage type names or wage type information from the payroll result, depending on the document grounding configuration.

Previously, this feature was available for Australia (English), Canada (French), Germany (German), Mexico (Spanish), Netherlands (Dutch), United Kingdom (English), and United States (English). The additions in this release are Brazil (Portuguese) and Canada (English).

Type: Changed | Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

Prerequisites: AI Units license, Joule enabled for SAP SuccessFactors, Explain Pay option enabled in AI Services Administration, pay statement (direct) integration enabled, and the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Enhanced Gender Field Replication

Employee Central Payroll now supports replication of Male and Female values from the country/region-specific gender field in Employee Central Global Information for all countries. Previously, replication from this field only worked for countries mapped in SAP's standard table (T77PAD_GENDER). For countries without mappings, customers had to use the standard gender field, which meant updating gender in two places.

With this change, Male and Female values are accepted for any country, even without a mapping in T77PAD_GENDER. Broader gender values such as non-binary are still replicated only for countries with SAP-delivered mappings. Unsupported values for unmapped countries will be blocked with an error message.

Type: Changed | Action: Required | Enablement: Automatically on

Prerequisite: Enable the country/region-specific gender field in Manage Business Configuration and assign a picklist that includes Male and Female values.

Employee Central Payroll User ID Generation from Identity Authentication Service

You can now generate the EC Payroll user ID from the external name provided by the Identity Authentication Service system. This works with EC Payroll on both SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP HCM. The system maps the Login Name in the Identity Provisioning Service to create the EC Payroll User ID. This decouples employee data replication from user synchronization, providing more flexibility in the identity management process.

Type: New | Enablement: Automatically on

Global User ID Supported for SAML Authentication

You can now use Global User ID as the primary identifier for SAML authentication in Employee Central Payroll based on SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA. This ensures compliance with identity and access management standards while improving integration capabilities.

Note that this feature is available only for new customers of EC Payroll based on SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA.

Type: New | Enablement: Customer configured

Replication of Start and End Times in Time Valuation Results

Start and end times in time valuation results are now replicated to the Employee Remuneration Info (2010) infotype. You can configure time valuation results in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking to include start times, end times, and a previous-day indicator, which are then replicated to the payroll system using the point-to-point integration.

This supports country-specific payroll requirements where start and end times are needed for accurate processing of wage types such as night premiums, Sunday premiums, and public holiday premiums. For example, German regulations under Section 3b of the Einkommensteuergesetz (Income Tax Law) require this information.

Type: New | Enablement: Customer configured

Prerequisite: Install the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Country-Specific Replication Enhancements

Several replication enhancements target specific countries in this release:

Norway: Work Capacity Percentage. The Available Work Capacity Percentage field can now be replicated from Employee Central to the Absences infotype (2001), field PSARB in EC Payroll. This field is used to calculate payments for employees unable to work at full capacity due to illness.

Sweden: Work Incapacity Percentage. Similarly, the Work Incapacity Percentage field is now replicated to the Absences infotype (2001) for Sweden, supporting accurate payment processing for partial absences due to illness.

Italy: Sickness Certificate Identifier. A new 20-character alphanumeric field for the sickness certificate identifier in the country-specific Time Off object for Italy is now replicated to the UMSCH field in the Absences infotype (2001). This value is included in the monthly UniEMENS report sent to INPS (the Italian national security body). Note that only absences created as of March 2026 can be replicated.

United Kingdom: Student Loan Plan Type 5. A new field for Student Loan Plan Type 5 from the Starter Checklist onboarding form can now be replicated to Tax Data GB (0065) and Court Orders GB (0070) infotypes.

New Zealand: KiwiSaver Onboarding Fields. The Savings Suspension End Date and Temporary Rate Reduction End Date fields from Section A of the KiwiSaver (KS2) onboarding form are now replicated to EC Payroll.

Canada: TD1 Compliance Forms. Canadian onboarding compliance form data (TD1 and province-specific variants) can now be automatically replicated from Onboarding to EC Payroll. This covers fields from infotype 0463 (Federal Tax CA), infotype 0461 (Tax Assignment CA), and infotype 0462 (Provincial Tax CA).

All replication enhancements require the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Legal Changes for Country/Region Specifics

The 1H 2026 release includes the following legal changes:

Country Description SAP Note(s)
EgyptIntegration of payroll with Egypt Tax Authority System (Unified Tax Procedure)3645008
SwitzerlandYear-end legal changes 2025/20263645577, 3706563, 3708154, 3703095, 3703501, 3657336, 3657337
PortugalLaw No. 104/2019: Reform of SIOE for Public Sector Reporting3701490, 3694988, 3700601, 3694334, 3690959, 3693907
NetherlandsYear-end legal changes 2025/20263664733, 3664710
GermanyIntroduction of KEA basic service for reduced hours compensation3602880
GermanyEEL new dataset version January 1, 20263634007
New ZealandEI Return Validations retrospective processing changes3693533
SingaporeMigration of IRAS IR21-API to Government's APEX3610023
BrazilLaw 15.270/2025: Withhold Income Tax (IRRF) Reduction3684446, 3687982, 3687983
TurkeySGK Min Wage and TAX Brackets changes for in-month changes3609544
United KingdomStatutory Parental Bereavement Pay updates for Northern Ireland, April 20263658742, 3681863, 3681864, 3693132, 3693133, 3693134

For more details on any legal change, use the Regulatory Change Manager: choose View All, then All Regulatory Changes, and filter by SAP SuccessFactors under Product and the relevant country.

Type: New | Classification: Major | Enablement: Customer configured

Important Note on Support Packages

Most features in this release require the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026. Until this support package is released, the features cannot be fully used. The schedules are published separately for SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP HCM (EA-HR EHP8). Plan your upgrade accordingly.

Summary

Here is a quick reference of all 14 EC Payroll features in the 1H 2026 release:

Feature Type Action Enablement
Basic Extensibility for Infotype 0000NewInfo onlyCustomer configured
Payroll Control Center EnhancementsChangedInfo onlyCustomer configured
AI-Assisted Explain My Pay Statement (Brazil, Canada)ChangedInfo onlyContact CEE/AM
Enhanced Gender Field ReplicationChangedRequiredAutomatically on
EC Payroll User ID from Identity AuthNewInfo onlyAutomatically on
Global User ID for SAML AuthNewInfo onlyCustomer configured
Start/End Times in Time Valuation to IT2010NewInfo onlyCustomer configured
Work Capacity Replication for NorwayNewInfo onlyAutomatically on
Work Incapacity Replication for SwedenNewInfo onlyAutomatically on
Sickness Certificate Replication for ItalyChangedInfo onlyAutomatically on
Student Loan Plan Type 5 for UKChangedInfo onlyAutomatically on
KiwiSaver Fields for New ZealandChangedInfo onlyAutomatically on
TD1 Compliance Forms for CanadaNewInfo onlyAutomatically on
Legal Changes for Country/Region SpecificsNewInfo onlyCustomer configured

Preview instances have features available as of April 13, 2026. Production availability is May 15, 2026. All features with replication dependencies require the May 2026 Synchronization HR Support Package.

Pay particular attention to the Enhanced Gender Field Replication feature, which carries a Required action classification and is Automatically On. Review your gender field configuration to ensure your picklists are set up correctly before the production release. 

This post is based on the publicly available SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 What's New documentation. Always refer to the official SAP documentation for implementation decisions.

entry.title: Re: Handling Dynamic Fields in SAP RAP using JSON Payload in CDS Custom Entity
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

In contrast to Standard CDS Views:

  • Custom Entities enable ABAP's dynamic data processing.
  • When logic cannot be managed only at the database level, they are usually employed.
  • They offer the freedom to execute intricate or unconventional scenarios, including external integrations or dynamic queries.

Because the data is fetched and processed in the ABAP layer, Custom Entities are well-suited for scenarios where:

  • Input parameters need to be interpreted dynamically
  • Data sources or structures vary at runtime
  • Standard CDS capabilities are not sufficient

"

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing again, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content:
I don't see anything specific in this code snippet nor can I reproduce the error in an more complete implementation. I would suggest getting an ODBC trace. In that trace, please ensure that the SQL_ATTR_ODBC_VERSION is set to SQL_OV_ODBC3 (SQLSetEnvAttr)  and then look for the 

DIAG [HY090] [Microsoft][ODBC Driver Manager] Invalid string or buffer length (0)

to confirm what ODBC function encounters the error. A call to SQLBindCol has a context for the error related to the BufferLength being not negative.  If it is coming from a different ODBC function, share what that function is and how your application has implemented it.

This really does not appear to be a SQLA driver issue. If you can share the trace, we can use ODBCTest to replay the ODBC calls leading to the error.

entry.title: Re: How can I connect my SAP BTP Kyma with my On-Premise...
entry.content: Hi @Aji , Good Day. Resurrecting this after a year as we have stumbled at the same roadblock. This link redirects us to the the connectivity proxy. However, there's no much documentation towards extending JCO to be used in the Kyma runtime. There's no specification on how the SAPJCO calls need to be routed via the connectivity proxy. Nor does SAPJCO seem to natively support proxying. We have been able to proxy the requests using tools like nginx's tcp routing but are stuck with connectivity-proxy throwing error "com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.server.proxy.InvalidHandshakePropertiesException: Destination host name not set " even though SAP JCO passes the "ashost" parameter. That 's the closest we have gotten.  We know the code works as it is successful on premise. We have also tried with hardcoding the destination parameters and at the same time configuring the destination as an RFC destination and using the SAP Cloud SDK to fetch destination details. FYI : We seem to have all the other components setup correctly eg: Cloud Connector with connectivity to on-premise , Connectivity Proxy module in Kyma, RFC destination.  We are reaching a point where we might need to consider a change in architecture but we would prefer not to if possible. Something like Kyma - HTTP -> Cloud Connector - HTTP -> Some new custom App in CloudFoundry on the lines on https://github.com/skye0402/jco-rfc-rest-server  - RFC -> Connectivity Service -> Cloud Connector -> On Prem . Since there seems to be a lot more doco for Cloud Foundry to on prem communication via RFC.  If you or any other Kyma experts on the forum have any guidance towards how to proceed invoking RFC protocol'd function modules using SAPJCO, it would be most appreciated. We suspect that its some configuration that we are missing or its just that SAP JCO is not supported or we are just integrating the wrong components together.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer error in SOIC sten
entry.content:
The issue is not because of stock but pricing. Perform the below steps

  1. Run transaction VA01 manually to create a sales order for the same customer/material and check pricing.
  2. Use Pricing Analysis (button in sales order) to see which condition types are missing.
  3. Maintain missing condition records in VK11.
  4. Once pricing is correct, the background process will generate the sales order successfully.

entry.title: Re: Can not use default value function for Sales Contract...
entry.content: The system does not support defaulting the Sales Contract Type field in Public Cloud. Having said that, it is not a configuration error on your side. Users will need to select it manually or use templates/variants as a workaround until SAP enables defaulting for this field.

entry.title: Re: EWM stock backdate
entry.content: Standard SAP does not provide backdated bin-level stock reporting. You’ll need to rely on material document history (standard reports like MB51 or MB5B)  for legal purposes or build a custom snapshot/reporting solution if bin-level detail is mandatory.

entry.title: Re: Sales Tax Calculation for Free Item and Tax amount to...
entry.content:
Spoiler
We tried to achieve by applying standard 100% discount condition after calculation of tax at item level. But this is driving the Order Item Net value (negative) -5 with Tax amount as 5. This cannot be submitted to GST.

From your above statement I believe, the step number for the above is including the tax condition type step number.  Just set the Step number of From-To of pr00 (that is only the price) to this 100% discount condition type, so that it will not impact the tax
